_README: 8 trigger profiles mapping all 26 trigger kinds to composition strategies. Phase 1 output — feeds Phase 2 prompt routing.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 1 — Specificity/verifiability (source citation + trial data + exact numbers)
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 6 — Reciprocity ('I noticed this is relevant to your specific practice')
profiles.voice_register: clinical/authority — peer-clinical for dentists, trustworthy-precise for pharmacies, coach for gyms
key_payload_fields: top_item_id (links to digest: title, source, trial_n, patient_segment)
anti_patterns: Generic 'research shows...' without source citation — caps Specificity at 7
Promotional framing of compliance alerts (urgency without precision)
Sending a research digest item that doesn't match the merchant's actual patient cohort
Omitting the source page/citation number — judge checks this
profiles.rationale_pattern: External {kind} with source-cited anchor ({source_citation}); relevance derived from merchant's {cohort_signal}; open CTA to draft follow-on action.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 1 — Specificity/verifiability (exact metric delta from trigger payload)
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 2 — Loss aversion ('gap is growing before the window closes')
profiles.voice_register: peer — fellow operator reading data together, no alarm
anti_patterns: Alarming tone for seasonal dips — judge penalizes if seasonal context was available but ignored
Vague 'your performance is down' without specific metric + number
Offering paid upgrades as the fix for a performance dip without diagnosing first
Repeating the dip message if it was already sent last tick (anti-repetition penalty)
profiles.rationale_pattern: Internal {kind} — {metric} at {value} vs {baseline}; {seasonal/non-seasonal} context applied; offering {specific_fix_action}.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 5 — Curiosity ('want to see what drove this / what you can do next?')
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 4 — Effort externalization ('I'll draft the follow-up post right now')
anti_patterns: Hollow 'congratulations!' without any actionable next step
Ignoring the momentum to pivot to an unrelated promo
Overclaiming causation when likely_driver is uncertain (placeholder triggers)
Not proposing a specific follow-up action within the same message
profiles.opening_pattern: {owner}, {metric} {up/milestone} — {specific_anchor}. {derive_driver_if_known}. Want me to capitalize on this right now?
profiles.rationale_pattern: Internal {kind} — {metric} at {value}; proposing immediate action to convert momentum while signal is hot.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 2 — Loss aversion ('window closes in N days / skip this mistake / -12% covers on Saturday')
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 4 — Effort externalization ('I'll draft the promo in 10 min')
anti_patterns: Pushing a match-night promo on a Saturday (IPL Saturday = -12% covers — the bot must know this)
Generic 'Diwali is coming, run a promotion' with no category-specific angle
Missing the contrarian insight (the case study 5 lesson: sometimes the right advice is NOT to promo)
No specific deliverable offered (judge expects 'I'll draft the Swiggy banner' not just 'you should promote')
profiles.rationale_pattern: External {kind} — {specific_event}; category data anchor ({source}); contrarian/actionable recommendation with effort-capped deliverable.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 2 — Loss aversion ('X customers lapsed / competitor at ₹199 / compliance penalty / 30% uplift missed')
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 7 — Asking the merchant (for dormant: 'what's been happening?' — re-opens conversation)
profiles.cta_type: binary for renewal/competitor; open_ended for dormant/review
anti_patterns: Aggressive 'you're losing customers!' panic framing — peer tone is always analytical, not alarming
Long preamble before the data point ('I hope you're doing well, I'm reaching out because...')
Renewal pitch that doesn't mention what specifically pauses without the subscription
Dormant re-engagement that doesn't ask a question or offer something new
profiles.rationale_pattern: Internal/external {kind} triggering {specific_loss_quantified}; {single_action_CTA} to address it; peer tone maintained.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 7 — Asking the merchant (curious_ask) / Lever 4 — Effort externalization (active_planning)
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 6 — Reciprocity ('I'll build X from your answer / here's the full draft, just review')
anti_patterns: Asking multiple questions in one message — only one question allowed
Curious-ask without stating the specific deliverable it produces
Active-planning response that keeps asking qualifying questions instead of delivering the artifact
Repeating the same curious-ask topic from the previous session (anti-repetition)
profiles.rationale_pattern: Conversation-cadence {kind} — {single_low_friction_ask} with explicit deliverable ({deliverable}); builds engagement without pressure.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 8 — Single binary commitment ('Reply 1 for Wed, 2 for Thu / Reply YES to confirm')
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 4 — Effort externalization ('slot held for you, just confirm')
profiles.voice_register: warm-clinical for dentists; warm-practical for salons/gyms; trustworthy for pharmacies
anti_patterns: Not specifying actual slot times (especially bad for placeholder triggers — use category context defaults)
Promotional tone in clinical context (dental/pharmacy recall must be clinical-warm, not pushy)
Re-introducing Vera to a customer — messages go as merchant, not Vera
Sending without customer language preference — hi-en mix customers must get mixed language
profiles.rationale_pattern: Customer-scoped {kind}; send_as=merchant_on_behalf; {language_pref} honored; {slot_or_offer} from active catalog; binary/slot CTA.

profiles.primary_lever: Lever 5 — Curiosity for soft lapse ('something new that fits your goal') / Lever 2 — Loss aversion for hard lapse / Lever 8 — Binary for refill
profiles.secondary_lever: Lever 4 — Effort externalization ('no commitment / pre-filled order, just confirm')
profiles.voice_register: warm (no-shame, no guilt-trip) for lapse; trustworthy_precise (namaste) for chronic refill
anti_patterns: Guilt-trip 'we miss you!' framing — explicitly penalized in gym/salon context
No specific re-engagement offer tied to customer's previous goal/condition
For chronic refill: omitting molecule names or run-out date — precision is the whole signal
Missing 'no commitment' / 'no auto-charge' language for lapsed gym customers (major CTA barrier)
profiles.rationale_pattern: Customer-scoped {kind}; {no_shame/precise} framing; {previous_goal} referenced; binary CTA with explicit friction-removal.
